title: Public Speaking

vocabulary.meaning: to speak in a calm, clear, and self-assured way
vocabulary.example: A good presenter can speak with confidence even under pressure.

vocabulary.meaning: to keep listeners interested and involved
vocabulary.example: Stories and questions can help a speaker engage the audience.

vocabulary.meaning: an organized beginning, middle, and end
vocabulary.example: A clear structure makes a speech easier to follow.

vocabulary.meaning: the way a person uses posture, gestures, and facial expressions
vocabulary.example: Positive body language can make a speaker look more professional.

vocabulary.meaning: to look at listeners while speaking
vocabulary.example: It is easier to maintain eye contact in a small room.

vocabulary.meaning: nervousness before or during speaking in front of people
vocabulary.example: Many students experience stage fright before their first presentation.

questions: Why or why not would you like to have a job that involves public speaking?
What are some jobs that involve public speaking?
Do you know any good public speakers? Who?
Do you know any bad public speakers? Who?
What makes a person a good public speaker?
What makes a person a bad public speaker?
